corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: Future work will investigate better-suited prior distributions, more flexible generative models and the application of a stochastic gradient descent algorithm for improved scalability.
1 A latent variable model for graph-structured data Figure : Latent space of unsupervised VGAE model trained on Cora citation network dataset .
1 A latent variable model for graph-structured data Figure : Latent space of unsupervised VGAE model trained on Cora citation network dataset .
We introduce the variational graph autoencoder (VGAE), a framework for unsupervised learning on graph-structured data based on the variational auto-encoder (VAE) .
We introduce the variational graph autoencoder (VGAE), a framework for unsupervised learning on graph-structured data based on the variational auto-encoder (VAE) .
Our model achieves competitive results on a link prediction task in citation networks.

In contrast to most existing models for unsupervised learning on graph-structured data and link prediction , our model can naturally incorporate node features, which significantly improves predictive performance on a number of benchmark datasets.

In contrast to most existing models for unsupervised learning on graph-structured data and link prediction , our model can naturally incorporate node features, which significantly improves predictive performance on a number of benchmark datasets.
We introduce an adjacency matrix A of G (we assume diagonal elements set to 1, i.e. every node is connected to itself) and its degree matrix D.
We further introduce stochastic latent variables z i , summarized in an N × F matrix Z.
The models are trained on an incomplete version of these datasets where parts of the citation links (edges) have been removed, while all node features are kept.
Nevertheless, the VGAE model achieves higher predictive performance on both the Cora and the Citeseer dataset.
Nevertheless, the VGAE model achieves higher predictive performance on both the Cora and the Citeseer dataset.
Future work will investigate better-suited prior distributions, more flexible generative models and the application of a stochastic gradient descent algorithm for improved scalability.
We report area under the ROC curve (AUC) and average precision (AP) scores for each model on the test set.
We report area under the ROC curve (AUC) and average precision (AP) scores for each model on the test set.
Numbers show mean results and standard error for 10 runs with random initializations on fixed dataset splits.
Adding input features significantly improves predictive performance across datasets.
